items.title: [Security] dangerouslyAllowNameMatching enables channel sender impersonation (V-03)
items.desc:
## Summary

When `channels.discord.dangerouslyAllowNameMatching=true` (or the Slack equivalent) is configured, the channel matches allowed senders by **display name** instead of opaque user ID. Display names are user-controlled and can be changed at any time, allowing an attacker to impersonate an authorized user.

items.title: fix(security): use constant-time comparison for TLS fingerprint verification
items.desc:
## Summary

TLS certificate fingerprint verification in `src/gateway/client.ts` uses `!==` (standard JavaScript string equality), which is vulnerable to **timing side-channel attacks** (CWE-208).

An attacker on the network path can measure response time differences to iteratively guess the correct fingerprint byte-by-byte, potentially bypassing TLS pinning for MITM attacks on gateway connections.

## Root cause

Two locations perform fingerprint comparison with `!==`:

items.title: Security: COMMAND_INJECTION in commands.py:974 (subprocess shell=True)
items.desc:
**Vulnerability:** COMMAND_INJECTION
**File:** aider/commands.py:974
**Severity:** Critical

**Vulnerable code:**
subprocess.run("git " + args, shell=True)

**Why it's dangerous:**
User-controlled git args passed to shell=True
→ /git status; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa possible
→ SSH key theft

**Fix:**
subprocess.run(["git"] + args.split(), shell=False)

**How I found it:**
pip install aina-scan
aina-vibeguard scan .

Found in 3 seconds.
Happy to submit a PR.

`SocketTransport.sendFrame` (the host-side `ncl` transport) has no request timeout and no bound on the response buffer. items.title: security fix(gateway): defend pre-auth device-signature verify against CPU DoS
items.desc:
Closes #77979

## Summary

- Pre-auth WS handshakes that include a `device` block ran `crypto.createPublicKey` plus a v3-then-v2 `crypto.verify` per request, gated only by frame-size limits.
